skill.name: frontend-responsive
skill.description: Build mobile-first responsive layouts with fluid containers, relative units, standard breakpoints, and touch-friendly design that adapts seamlessly across devices. Use this skill when creating or modifying layouts, implementing media queries, defining breakpoints, choosing sizing units, optimizing for mobile devices, or testing UI across screen sizes. Apply when working with responsive design, mobile layouts, tablet views, desktop views, viewport configuration, or any styling that needs to adapt to different screen sizes and device capabilities.
skill.summary: Guide agents to build responsive frontend layouts that work across mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found repeated high-severity filesystem and keyword patterns in SKILL.md. The cryptography and reconnaissance matches are false positives from normal responsive design wording, but the relative path traversal link on line 29 is a real packaging concern because it directs the agent outside the skill file.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package Relative File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/responsive.md. This is not confirmed malware, but it directs the agent outside the packaged skill boundary and could load unreviewed instructions if the surrounding repository changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is directly present in the markdown link. The surrounding text frames it as a reference file, so the concern is unreviewed instruction loading rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns on SKILL.md lines 3, 13, 15, 16, and 24. These lines contain responsive design guidance and a Claude Code description, with no evidence of cryptographic operations, command execution, system probing, network access, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language documentation about responsive layouts. I found no evidence in SKILL.md of cryptographic APIs, shell commands, reconnaissance behavior, or prompt injection instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal Sequence
dangerous_patterns.description: The markdown reference on line 29 uses ../../../ to resolve content outside the skill directory. In a marketplace skill, this can bypass review of the complete instruction set.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is explicit and repeated in the static findings. It appears in a documentation link, so it is risky packaging rather than direct filesystem exploitation.

content.value_statement: Responsive layouts often fail when spacing, breakpoints, and touch targets are handled late. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward mobile-first frontend decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides mobile-first layout planning for frontend pages and components.
Recommends fluid containers, relative units, and standard responsive breakpoints.
Helps choose sizing units such as rem, em, percent, vw, and vh.
Supports testing and debugging layouts across mobile, tablet, and desktop widths.
Promotes touch-friendly targets and content priority for smaller screens.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include executable validation tools.
It depends on a referenced standards file outside the packaged skill.
It does not define framework-specific component implementations.
It cannot verify visual results without a separate browser or screenshot workflow.

use_cases.title: Create a Mobile-First Product Page
use_cases.description: Use the skill to plan layout hierarchy, breakpoints, spacing, and touch targets before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review a Responsive Component
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check whether a component adapts cleanly across phone, tablet, and desktop widths.

use_cases.title: Improve Design System Layout Rules
use_cases.description: Use the skill to align spacing, typography, and container behavior with responsive design standards.

prompt_templates.title: Make This Layout Responsive
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single screen or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this frontend layout so it works well on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Use mobile-first CSS and explain the breakpoint choices.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Responsive Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for responsive issues. Check containers, typography, spacing, media queries, touch targets, and content priority.

prompt_templates.title: Design Breakpoint Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose a breakpoint and sizing strategy for this interface. Include container widths, relative units, and changes for each viewport range.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Complex Responsive View
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these components into a cohesive responsive system. Preserve behavior, reduce layout duplication, and verify mobile, tablet, and desktop states.

output_examples.input: A dashboard has fixed-width cards that overflow on phones.
output_examples.output: The agent would recommend fluid grid columns, mobile-first stacking, constrained card widths, and tablet and desktop breakpoints.

output_examples.input: A form uses small controls and dense spacing on mobile.
output_examples.output: The agent would suggest larger touch targets, clearer vertical spacing, responsive labels, and simplified content order for narrow screens.

output_examples.input: A marketing page needs responsive typography.
output_examples.output: The agent would propose relative font units, stable line lengths, breakpoint-specific spacing, and testing across common viewport widths.

best_practices: Start with the smallest viewport, then add layout complexity at larger breakpoints.
Use relative units and fluid containers unless a fixed size is required.
Test important flows at mobile, tablet, laptop, and wide desktop widths.
anti_patterns: Do not depend on fixed desktop widths for primary content.
Do not hide critical actions only because the viewport is small.
Do not rely on viewport scaling that makes text unreadable.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents make frontend layouts responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.

faq.question: Does it generate CSS by itself?
faq.answer: It guides the agent during coding, but the final CSS depends on the project and prompt.

faq.question: Can it work with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill mentions responsive utilities and can guide Tailwind breakpoint usage.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish as packaged?
faq.answer: Not without review. It references a file outside the packaged skill directory.

faq.question: Does it include visual testing tools?
faq.answer: No. Use a browser, screenshots, or automated visual tests to verify results.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend developers, UI engineers, and design system maintainers working on responsive interfaces.
